name: Gallium Nitride Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Gallium Nitride. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Controlled power for precision semiconductor cleaning

pulseWidth.description: Ultra-short pulses for minimal thermal damage

power.description: Typical range for pulsed fiber lasers in semiconductor cleaning; starting at 50 W balances efficiency and safety for GaN's sensitivity to heat (thermal conductivity ~130 W/mK); max avoids melting point (~2500°C). Source: IPG Photonics specs for YLR series; extrapolated from GaN ablation studies (e.g., J. Appl. Phys. 2015).

scan_speed.description: Effective for light contaminants at higher speeds, slower for heavy; 1000 mm/s optimal for uniform GaN surface prep without overheating. Range from industry standards for 1064 nm cleaning. Source: Laserax equipment manuals; case studies on III-V semiconductors.

pulse_width.description: Picosecond preferred over ns for minimal HAZ on reflective GaN (reflectivity ~40% at 1064 nm); avoids thermal accumulation. Femtosecond possible but less common industrially. Source: Laser physics research (Opt. Express 2018 on ultrashort pulse cleaning of nitrides).

repetition_rate.description: 50 kHz balances speed and heat management for GaN; higher rates risk accumulation on low-absorptivity material, slower for precision. Trade-off: faster cleaning but potential thermal damage. Source: Academic literature (Appl. Surf. Sci. 2020 on pulsed laser cleaning of GaN).

energy_density.description: Below GaN damage threshold (~1-2 J/cm² at 1064 nm ns pulses) for safe contaminant removal; 0.5 J/cm² recommended for light industrial cleaning. Conservative for GaN's high melting point but low IR absorption. Source: Ablation threshold studies (J. Phys. D: Appl. Phys. 2017).

spot_size.description: 200 μm for fine GaN surface prep (e.g., LED substrates); smaller for detail, larger for area coverage. Matches typical Gaussian beam in cleaning heads. Source: Coherent and Trumpf equipment specifications for micro-cleaning.

pass_count.description: Multi-pass (2 recommended) for complete removal of heavy contaminants without single-pass overload on delicate GaN; single pass for light. Based on industrial practice for semiconductors. Source: Case studies in Surf. Coat. Technol. 2019.

overlap_ratio.description: 60% ensures uniform coverage on GaN without excessive energy overlap causing damage; typical range for pulsed scanning. Optimizes for even ablation of contaminants. Source: Process optimization studies (Opt. Lasers Eng. 2021).
